reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (25% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 15/100 [Critical risk] — 6 high, 1 critical, 1 medium, 1 low

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (15/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-3] Autonomous moderation with minimal human oversight — no human-in-the-loop controls or kill switch

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 15/100 (critical risk) — Δ35 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 15/100 (critical risk) — Δ84 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Base score: 55/100 average across 3 module(s)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
VeriMedia AI presents a deeply concerning risk posture characterized by two compounding problems: a complete absence of governance, compliance, and documentation artifacts (comprehensively cataloged by Guardian with a critical 15/100 score across nine findings), and a simultaneous inability to assess technical security due to no source code being provided (independently confirmed by both Sentinel and Watchdog). The most alarming finding is Guardian's [guardian-3] identification of autonomous content moderation with no human-in-the-loop controls, no kill switch, and no escalation path — a critical deficiency for a system that directly affects public discourse and would almost certainly be classified as high-risk under the EU AI Act. This is compounded by the absence of bias testing [guardian-2], audit logging [guardian-6], explainability [guardian-5], and privacy controls [guardian-7], creating a system that makes consequential automated decisions with no accountability infrastructure.

A key tension in the assessment is the divergence between Watchdog's 99/100 score and Guardian's 15/100 score. While Watchdog correctly notes it found no evidence of LLM-specific vulnerabilities, its near-perfect score is misleading for a system where the entire codebase is unexamined. The Arbiter recommends that all modules treat unanalyzed attack surfaces as elevated risk rather than as absence of risk, particularly for a platform whose stated purpose involves adversarial use cases (detecting manipulated media implies motivated adversaries). Critical gaps remain unaddressed across all three modules: adversarial ML robustness testing (evasion and poisoning attacks against the ResNet/BERT models), ML model supply-chain integrity (trojaned weights from public repositories), and AWS infrastructure security (IAM, S3, encryption, network segmentation). The immediate priority must be threefold: (1) provide source code and deployment artifacts to enable Sentinel and Watchdog analysis, (2) implement human oversight controls and a kill switch before any production deployment per Guardian's [guardian-3] finding, and (3) conduct a comprehensive risk assessment covering the adversarial ML and infrastructure gaps identified by this Arbiter review.

top_findings: Insufficient source code provided for static analysis
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask/Python-based media verification platform using ResNet/BERT models, PostgreSQL, and AWS deployment. No source code, dependency manifests, or entry points were provided, so a meaningful static-security assessment could not be performed. High-risk areas to prioritise when source is available include authentication/session handling, secret management, file-upload and input validation, and deployment TLS/configuration.
sentinel.recommendation: 1) Provide the repository (app entrypoints, config, requirements) so Sentinel can perform full static analysis — this addresses the 'Insufficient source code' finding. 2) Once code is provided, immediately run automated security scans: bandit (Python), pip-audit or safety for dependency vulnerabilities, and Trivy for container images; include results with the repo. 3) Prioritise manual review of endpoints that accept user uploads or moderation decisions (files handling, auth middleware, DB queri

top_findings: No LLM/LLM-integration code available for analysis (VeriMedia AI)
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI — no LLM/LLM-integration source code was provided. The application description indicates ML components (ResNet, BERT) and autonomous moderation, but the supplied artifacts contained no files to search for LLM SDKs or prompt usage. Therefore no concrete OWASP-LLM findings could be produced from code evidence.
watchdog.recommendation:
Top 3 priority remediation actions:
1) Provide source code or deployment artifacts immediately (include files that may contain LLM usage such as any Python modules importing openai, langchain, llama_index, anthropic, cohere, transformers, or other LLM SDKs). This will allow a line-by-line OWASP-LLM assessment (prompt injection, RAG poisoning, tool misuse, etc.).
2) If LLMs are in use or planned, implement immediate defensive controls before public deployment: enforce strict input validation and

top_findings: No high-level governance documentation (README, policies, model card)
No bias and fairness testing for ResNet (deepfake) and BERT (fact-checking) models
Autonomous moderation with minimal human oversight — no human-in-the-loop controls or kill switch
guardian.summary: VeriMedia AI is an AI-powered media verification platform (Flask API, ResNet CV models, BERT NLP, PostgreSQL, AWS) with an overall critically weak governance posture. The repository contains no governance artifacts (README, model card, privacy policy), no bias/fairness testing, no human-in-the-loop controls for automated moderation, and no regulatory conformity documentation — exposing the project to regulatory non-compliance and high risk of harmful disparate outcomes.
guardian.recommendation: 1) Implement immediate human oversight and emergency controls: create governance/human_oversight.md, implement a human review queue and a kill switch (EUAI-OVERSIGHT). 2) Publish core documentation and model provenance: add README.md, docs/model-card.md, docs/model-provenance.md, and models/manifest.json with checksums and licenses (NIST-GOV, NIST-MAP). 3) Run and document bias/fairness and privacy assessments: add tests/bias_tests.py, docs/fairness_assessment.md, privacy_policy.md, and a DPIA a
